2604.14116.title: TREX: Automating LLM Fine-tuning via Agent-Driven Tree-based Exploration
2604.14116.paper_summary: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have empowered AI research agents to perform isolated scientific tasks, automating complex, real-world workflows, such as LLM training, remains a significant challenge. In this paper, we introduce TREX, a multi-agent system that automates the entire LLM training life-cycle. By orchestrating collaboration between two core modules-the Researcher and the Executor-the system seamlessly performs requirement analysis, open-domain literature and data research, formulation of training strategies, preparation of data recipes, and model training and evaluation. The multi-round experimental process is modeled as a search tree, enabling the system to efficiently plan exploration paths, reuse historical results, and distill high-level insights from iterative trials. To evaluate the capability of automated LLM training, we construct FT-Bench, a benchmark comprising 10 tasks derived from real-world scenarios, ranging from optimizing fundamental model capabilities to enhancing performance on domain-specific tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that the TREX agent consistently optimizes model performance on target tasks.
2604.14116.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）雖然使 AI 研究代理能夠執行孤立的科學任務，但自動化複雜的現實世界工作流程，例如 LLM 訓練，仍然是一項重大挑戰。<lb/>在本文中，我們介紹了 TREX，一個自動化整個 LLM 訓練生命週期的多代理系統。<lb/>通過協調兩個核心模塊——研究者和執行者之間的合作，該系統無縫地執行需求分析、開放領域文獻和數據研究、訓練策略的制定、數據配方的準備，以及模型訓練和評估。<lb/>多輪實驗過程被建模為搜索樹，使系統能夠有效規劃探索路徑、重用歷史結果，並從迭代試驗中提煉高層次的見解。<lb/>為了評估自動化 LLM 訓練的能力，我們構建了 FT-Bench，一個包含 10 個來自現實世界場景的任務的基準，這些任務範圍從優化基本模型能力到提高特定領域任務的性能。<lb/>實驗結果表明，TREX 代理在目標任務上持續優化模型性能。
2604.14116.author: Zerun Ma et.al.
2604.14116.authors: Zerun Ma, Guoqiang Wang, Xinchen Xie, Yicheng Chen, He Du, Bowen Li, Yanan Sun, Wenran Liu, Kai Chen, Yining Li
